summary.narrative: Gov. Pritzker campaigned on job creation, budget balancing, and freedom protection. Second-term accomplishments include doubling private investments ($6.3B to $12.5B, 2023-2024), assault weapons ban (2023), nuclear energy policy shifts, infrastructure via Rebuild Illinois ($9.6B invested), healthcare expansion, and K-12 funding increases. Recent executive orders address autism rights and accountability. Mixed record: strong economic metrics versus complex policy reversals on nuclear development.
summary.grade_rationale: Delivered measurable economic growth and signature legislation on gun safety; healthcare/education investment occurred. However, nuclear policy shifts suggest initial platform ambiguity. Limited transparency on specific 2018 campaign commitments versus recent achievements.

promises.normalized: Double private sector investments in Illinois
Ban assault weapons and high-capacity magazines
Expand healthcare access for vulnerable populations
Increase K-12 school funding to record levels

data_gaps: Original 2018 campaign platform specifics unavailable; analysis based on 2023-2025 accomplishments
Election margin data for 2022 gubernatorial race not provided in sources
Quantified metrics for healthcare expansion and K-12 funding increases lacking
Details on 'protecting freedoms' campaign messaging remain vague
Nuclear policy evolution rationale unclear; appears to reverse earlier support
